content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Executive/Legal · · Enforcement · · Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660              322-5901           322-6441              322-6444

March 27, 1984


Cleo Moore
P.O. Box 7
North San Juan, CA 95960

Re: Advice Letter No. A-84-036

Dear Ms. Moore:

     Thank you for your request for advice on the conflict of
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act. The following
facts were provided in your letter and during my telephone
conversation with you on March 7, 1984:

FACTS

     On November 25, 1983, you were appointed Director of the San
Juan Ridge County Water District. You resigned the position at
the suggestion of the Nevada County Counsel's Office, which felt
that you had a potential conflict of interest due to your
employment by the California Green Thumb. The Green Thumb is a
nonprofit organization which provides work for senior citizens.
Your job title is "crew foreman" and your function is to oversee
work crews. One of the entities for whom the Green Thumb crews
perform work is the North San Juan Ridge County Water District.
The crew's services for the District include cleaning ditches of
debris, checking for vandalism, monitoring the amount of water
used, and handling much of the office work. You are paid a
minimum wage for up to 20 hours of work per week. These wages
are paid by the program, which is federally subsidized, and not
by the entities for whom your crew work. Your supervisors
arrange for the jobs that your crew performs.

QUESTION PRESENTED

     Do you have a conflict of interest that prevents you from
serving as Director of the San Juan Ridge County Water District?

Cleo Moore
March 27, 1984
Page 2


CONCLUSION

     The Political Reform Act does not prevent you from serving
as the Director of the Water District.  However, it may affect
your governmental decisionmaking as Director.

DISCUSSION

     The conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform
Act do not prohibit you from serving as the Director of the
Water District.  However, the Act's provisions may affect your
decisionmaking as Director.  Government Code Section 87100
prohibits a local public official from making or participating
in the making of a governmental decision in which she knows or
has reason to know that she has a financial interest.  An
official has a "financial interest" in a decision within the
meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that
the decision will have a material financial effect./ on:

     * * *

     (c)  Any source of income . . . aggregating two
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided
to . . . the public official within 12 months prior to
. . . the decision is made.....

                                    (Section 87103(c).)

     If, as the Director of the Water District, you are confronted
with a decision that will have a material financial effect on
Green Thumb, you must disqualify yourself because it is a source
of income to you.  A decision is said to have a "material"
effect on a nonprofit organization if the decision's effect will
be "substantial."  (See 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18702(b)(3)(D).)  If you have difficulty in determining whether
your disqualification is required on a particular decision, you
can contact our office for advice.

     The County Counsel's Office is concerned that, as the
Director of the Water District, you may be confronted with

___________________

     1/  Hereinafter all statutory references are to the
Government Code, unless otherwise indicated.

     2/  For an explanation of the phrase "material financial
effect," see the enclosed copy 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702.

Cleo Moore
March 27, 1984
Page 3


decisions which will affect the amount of work and, hence, the
amount of money, that you receive from the Green Thumb. Under
the Act, you are required to disqualify yourself from decisions
which will directly increase your income by $100 or more.
(2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702(b)(3)(A).) This means that you
must disqualify yourself if you know or have reason to know that
a decision will result in your personally receiving sufficient
additional hours of work such that your income will be increased
by at least $100.

     My advice is confined to the provisions of the Political
Reform Act. You should contact your County Counsel's Office
concerning the possible application of Section 1090, which is
outside the FPPC's jurisdiction. If you have any additional
questions concerning the Political Reform Act, please feel free
to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        Janis Shank McLean
                                        Janis Shank McLean
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

JSM:plh
Enclosures

February 17th 1987

FPPC
Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
1100 K Street Sacramento, California 95814

FEB 20 1 23 PM '04

Dear Barbara Wilnew;

This is to confirm my phone call to your office/secretary February 10th 1987 Spoke to a Betty Brown secretary to Jeanne Pritchard. A gentleman called me from your organization on February 14th.87 I gave him a run down on why I asked for a ruling on a possible conflict of interest. It said it send all pertinent information to you so here goes:

I was appointed to the Office of Director, San Juan Ridge County Water District by the Nevada County Board of Supervisors in lieu of election for a four year term effective November 26th 1983. I filed a Certificate of Appointment and Oath of Office plus a Statement of Economic interest at the County Clerks office and with the Clerk of the Board of Supervisors on December 9th 1984. Shortly after filing became a member of the county councils office about a possible conflict of interest and he suggested that I begin [illegible] further investigation so I did resign my position as Director in December 10th 1984.

Wrote to the California Beca Turnd asking for a ruling and received a letter of which you have a carbon copy from Regina Solome kitchens State Director - with your address. Letter dated 1-30-84

do of one well up for a feeling re my having
to resign from the Board of Directors of the
Water District, because of a possible conflict
of interest, being a director and employed by
the Aeon Thumb. The reasons being that if
Aeon on some contractor another it would
possibly be something that would give me
more work in my capacity as a Aeon Thumb
worker.

If the District ever has a major problem
that calls for a contract we get bids and
let out the contract which in no way
would cause more work for those of us
who work for Aeon Thumb. Most of the major
things are taken care of by plumbers from the
District as it has been for many years.

The only thing that we used by the
Aeon Thumb workers are Hand Dole and
a tee weed setter & chain saws Enclosed
find copy of Duties for the Aeon Thumb
workers for the Water District

I thank you for your
attention to this matter,

Sincerely,
Cleo H. Meek
P.O. Box 7
North San Juan
Ca. 95960

COUNTY OF NEVADA
STATE OF CALIFORNIA                    BOARD OF SUPERVISORS

Ilse E. Bornhardt, 1st District
Robert H. Wilder, 2nd District
Karsten Hansen, 3rd District
Eric W. Rood, 4th District
Patricia S. Sutton, 5th District
                                       201 Church Street
Cathy R. Thompson                      NEVADA CITY, CALIFORNIA 95959
Clerk of the Board                     Phone: Area Code 916-265-1480


                                       November 30, 1983


Cleo H. Moore
P.O. Box 7
North San Juan, CA 95960

Dear Mrs. Moore:

On October 3, 1983, the Nevada County Board of Supervisors adopted
Resolution 83-383, authorizing appointments in lieu of election to
special district boards. This action approved your appointment to
the office of Director, San Juan Ridge County Water District, for
a four-year term, effective November 25, 1983.

Transmitted herewith please find a "Certificate of Appointment and
Oath of Office" and "Statement of Economic Interest" to be completed
and returned to this office prior to your voting as a Director and
no later than December 25, 1983. The oath of office certificate
must be executed either in the presence of a Deputy County Clerk or
Notary Public.

Thank you for your cooperation in this matter and congratulations
on your appointment.

                                       Very truly yours,

                                       [signature]
                                       CATHY R. THOMPSON
                                       Clerk of the Board

/ct

cc: San Juan Ridge County Water District

SAN JUAN RIDGE COUNTY WATER DISTRICT
20342 Pleasant Valley Road
No. San Juan, California 95960

CLEAN DITCHES OF DEBRIS
CUT BRUSH--BURN WHEN ALLOWABLE
TURN ON WATER & monitor this to each users box
CHECK FOR LEAKS IN DITCHES and/or RESERVOIRS
CHECK FOR VANDALISM
KEEP TRACK OF USERS AMOUNTS OF WATER
KEEP LATERAL DITCHES CLEAR & OPEN for free flow of
irrigation water delivery
TAKE CARE OF OFFICE FILINGS MAKE CUT BILLS for the
water; DO ALL BOOK-KEEPING WRITING LETTERS
for the DISTRICT
SEE BOXES ARE CLOSED AFTER EACH WATER USERS TURN IS
over, so the next USER will set his share
MAINTAIN TOOLS, keeping in good working condition
INSPECT GATE ON DIVERSION DAM (SHADY CREEK) for
leaks or vandalism
CHECK MEASURING BOX AT PINE GROVE RESERVOIR for any
damage from Cattle & Horses in pasture while
water is in the delivery ditch
REPAIR ANY DAMAGE TO DITCH, BOXES, FLUME, & MEASUR-
ING DEVICES
CHECK FOR UNAUTHORIZED DIVERSIONS BY PEOPLE OUT OF
DISTRICT, but on the ditch, from SHADY CREEK
TO PINE GROVE RESERVOIR




"Work duties for S.J.R. Co. Water District"
